title: AlphaEvolve: A coding agent for scientific and algorithmic discovery
abstract: We introduce AlphaEvolve, an evolutionary coding agent that substantially enhances capabilities of state-of-the-art LLMs on highly challenging tasks such as tackling open scientific problems or optimizing critical pieces of computational infrastructure. AlphaEvolve orchestrates an autonomous pipeline of LLMs, whose task is to improve an algorithm by making direct changes to the code. Using an evolutionary approach, continuously receiving feedback from evaluators, AlphaEvolve iteratively improves algorithms, potentially leading to new scientific and practical discoveries.
relevance: Foundational paper for YGN-SAGE's evolution engine. AlphaEvolve's evolutionary pipeline with LLM-driven code mutation, evaluator feedback, and iterative improvement is the direct inspiration for the DGM/SAMPO evolution system. Key techniques: multi-model ensemble (Gemini Flash + Pro), evolutionary search with code diffs, automated evaluation.

title: CodeEvolve: an open source evolutionary coding agent for algorithm discovery and optimization
abstract: CodeEvolve is an open-source evolutionary coding agent inspired by AlphaEvolve, designed for algorithm discovery and optimization. It provides a reproducible and accessible framework for evolutionary code optimization, enabling researchers to build upon and extend the evolutionary coding paradigm without proprietary dependencies.
relevance: Open-source implementation of AlphaEvolve principles. Directly relevant as a reference architecture for YGN-SAGE's evolution engine. Provides insights on making evolutionary coding frameworks modular and extensible, which aligns with YGN-SAGE's ADK philosophy.

title: Evolution through Large Models
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) trained on code can vastly improve the effectiveness of mutation operators applied to programs in genetic programming. This paper pursues the insight that LLMs, having been trained on data that includes sequential changes and modifications, can approximate likely changes that humans would make. The approach is demonstrated by combining LLMs with MAP-Elites to generate hundreds of thousands of functional Python programs that output working ambulating robots in the Sodarace domain.
relevance: Seminal paper establishing LLMs as mutation operators for evolutionary computation. Directly inspired YGN-SAGE's LLM-driven code mutation (llm_mutator.py) and the integration of MAP-Elites with LLM-based evolution. The diff-based mutation model is foundational to YGN-SAGE's approach.

title: ReEvo: Large Language Models as Hyper-Heuristics with Reflective Evolution
abstract: Language Hyper-Heuristics (LHHs) leverage LLMs for heuristic generation with minimal manual intervention and open-ended heuristic spaces. ReEvo presents Reflective Evolution, a generic searching framework that emulates the reflective design approach of human experts. It integrates evolutionary search for efficiently exploring the heuristic space and uses LLM reflections to provide verbal gradients within the space. Across five algorithmic types and six combinatorial optimization problems, ReEvo yields state-of-the-art meta-heuristics.
relevance: Highly relevant to YGN-SAGE's evolution engine. ReEvo's 'reflective evolution' concept -- using LLM self-reflection as verbal gradients to guide evolutionary search -- could enhance the SAMPO action selection and DGM directive system. The hyper-heuristic framing maps to YGN-SAGE's strategy pillar. Published at NeurIPS 2024.

title: Automated Design of Agentic Systems
abstract: We formalize and begin to study the newly forming research area of Automated Design of Agentic Systems (ADAS), which aims to automatically create powerful agentic system designs. We present Meta Agent Search, where a meta agent iteratively programs interesting new agents based on an ever-growing archive of previous discoveries. Since programming languages are Turing Complete, this approach theoretically enables the learning of any possible agentic system. Agents invented by Meta Agent Search greatly outperform hand-designed agents.
relevance: Core ADK paper directly relevant to YGN-SAGE's self-evolving agent architecture. Meta Agent Search's archive-based evolutionary discovery of agent designs parallels YGN-SAGE's topology evolution via MAP-Elites. The concept of automatically designing agentic systems in code validates YGN-SAGE's approach of agents creating their own topology. ICLR 2025.

title: A Self-Improving Coding Agent
abstract: Recent advancements in Large Language Models (LLMs) have spurred interest in deploying LLM agents to undertake tasks in the world. LLMs are often deployed in agent systems: code that orchestrates LLM calls and provides them with tools. We demonstrate that an agent system, equipped with basic coding tools, can autonomously edit itself and improve its performance on benchmark tasks. Performance gains range from 17% to 53% on SWE Bench Verified, with additional gains on LiveCodeBench.
relevance: Directly validates YGN-SAGE's core thesis: agents that can self-modify their own code improve performance. The self-editing mechanism parallels YGN-SAGE's evolution engine where agents modify their own topology and tools. The 17-53% improvement on SWE-bench demonstrates practical viability of self-improvement.

title: Live-SWE-agent: Can Software Engineering Agents Self-Evolve on the Fly?
abstract: Large Language Models are reshaping software engineering through autonomous agents equipped with coding tools. While existing software agents show promise, they require dedicated design and may be suboptimal. Live-SWE-agent represents the first live software agent capable of autonomously and continuously evolving itself on-the-fly during runtime when solving real-world software problems. Starting with minimal scaffolding, it achieves a 77.4% solve rate on SWE-bench Verified.
relevance: Breakthrough in runtime self-evolution for coding agents. Directly relevant to YGN-SAGE's evolution pillar -- demonstrates that agents can evolve during task execution, not just between runs. The 77.4% SWE-bench solve rate from minimal scaffolding validates the power of self-evolution over hand-designed architectures.

title: OpenHands: An Open Platform for AI Software Developers as Generalist Agents
abstract: Software is one of the most powerful tools that we humans have at our disposal; it allows a skilled programmer to interact with the world in complex and profound ways. We introduce OpenHands (f.k.a. OpenDevin), a platform for the development of powerful and flexible AI agents that interact with the world in similar ways to those of a human developer: by writing code, interacting with a command line, and browsing the web. The platform includes agent hub with 10+ agents, evaluation framework, and interaction mechanisms.
relevance: Major open-source ADK for software agents. OpenHands' CodeAct architecture (code as the unified action space) parallels YGN-SAGE's tools pillar. The agent hub concept and evaluation framework provide design patterns for YGN-SAGE's agent pool system. Published at ICLR 2025.

title: The OpenHands Software Agent SDK: A Composable and Extensible Foundation for Production Agents
abstract: Agents are now used widely in the process of software development, but building production-ready software engineering agents is a complex task. Deploying software agents effectively requires flexibility in implementation and experimentation, reliable and secure execution, and interfaces for users to interact with agents. We present the OpenHands Software Agent SDK, a toolkit for implementing software development agents that satisfy these desiderata.
relevance: Provides composable SDK architecture for agent development -- directly comparable to YGN-SAGE's Python SDK design. Key patterns: composable agent building blocks, secure sandboxed execution (parallels YGN-SAGE's eBPF/Wasm sandbox), and extensible tool interfaces.

title: EvoAgent: Towards Automatic Multi-Agent Generation via Evolutionary Algorithms
abstract: The rise of powerful large language models (LLMs) has spurred a new trend in building LLM-based autonomous agents for solving complex tasks, especially multi-agent systems. Despite the remarkable progress, existing works are heavily dependent on human-designed frameworks, which greatly limits the functional scope and scalability of agent systems. EvoAgent is a generic method to automatically extend specialized agents to multi-agent systems via evolutionary algorithms, applying mutation, crossover, selection operators to generate diverse agents.
relevance: Directly relevant to YGN-SAGE's agent_pool.py and topology evolution. EvoAgent's evolutionary operators (mutation, crossover, selection) for generating multi-agent configurations parallel YGN-SAGE's MAP-Elites topology search. The automatic scaling from single to multi-agent validates YGN-SAGE's dynamic sub-agent pool design.

title: Illuminating search spaces by mapping elites
abstract: Nearly all science and engineering fields use search algorithms, which automatically explore a search space to find high-performing solutions. Yet, the current search paradigm treats the search space as a black box, which severely limits our ability to understand the relationship between solution properties and performance. We present a new algorithm, MAP-Elites, that provides a holistic view of how high-performing solutions are distributed throughout a search space by creating a map of elites at each point in a user-defined space of dimensions of variation.
relevance: Foundational paper for YGN-SAGE's evo_topology.py MAP-Elites implementation. This seminal QD algorithm directly underpins the evolutionary topology search system, providing the archive-based diversity maintenance mechanism that enables YGN-SAGE to explore diverse agent topologies while maintaining quality.

title: Covariance Matrix Adaptation for the Rapid Illumination of Behavior Space
abstract: We focus on the challenge of finding a diverse collection of quality solutions on complex continuous domains. While quality diversity (QD) algorithms like MAP-Elites are designed to generate a diverse range of solutions, these algorithms require a large number of evaluations for exploration of continuous spaces. We introduce CMA-ME (Covariance Matrix Adaptation MAP-Elites), combining CMA-ES self-adaptation techniques with archiving for maintaining diversity.
relevance: CMA-ME extends MAP-Elites with efficient continuous-space exploration via covariance matrix adaptation. Could enhance YGN-SAGE's evo_topology.py with more sample-efficient exploration of the topology parameter space, especially for continuous hyperparameter dimensions. The emitter-based architecture is a proven upgrade path.

title: Differentiable Quality Diversity
abstract: Quality diversity (QD) is a growing branch of stochastic optimization research that studies the problem of generating an archive of solutions that maximize a given objective function but are also diverse with respect to a set of specified measure functions. However, even when these functions are differentiable, QD algorithms treat them as black boxes, ignoring gradient information. We present the Differentiable Quality Diversity (DQD) problem and introduce MAP-Elites via a Gradient Arborescence (MEGA).
relevance: Introduces gradient-informed QD optimization. Could be integrated into YGN-SAGE's evolution system when objective/measure functions are differentiable (e.g., neural architecture parameters). The MEGA algorithm provides a path to more efficient MAP-Elites when gradients are available through the LLM.

title: Voyager: An Open-Ended Embodied Agent with Large Language Models
abstract: We introduce Voyager, the first LLM-powered embodied lifelong learning agent in Minecraft that continuously explores the world, acquires diverse skills, and makes novel discoveries without human intervention. Voyager consists of three key components: 1) an automatic curriculum that maximizes exploration, 2) an ever-growing skill library of executable code for storing and retrieving complex behaviors, and 3) a new iterative prompting mechanism that incorporates environment feedback, execution errors, and self-verification.
relevance: Pioneer of LLM-powered lifelong learning agents with skill libraries. Voyager's ever-growing skill library of executable code directly parallels YGN-SAGE's tools pillar and memory system. The automatic curriculum maps to YGN-SAGE's strategy/metacognition system, and iterative prompting with self-verification maps to the S2 AVR loop.

title: SuperCoder: Assembly Program Superoptimization with Large Language Models
abstract: Superoptimization transforms programs into faster versions while preserving input-output behavior. This work investigates whether LLMs can serve as superoptimizers, generating assembly programs that outperform compiler-optimized code. We construct the first large-scale benchmark with 8,072 assembly programs averaging 130 lines. Evaluating 23 LLMs, claude-opus achieved 51.5% test-passing rate and 1.43x speedup over gcc -O3. Fine-tuned SuperCoder with RL attained 95.0% correctness and 1.46x average speedup.
relevance: Demonstrates LLM-driven code superoptimization achieving 1.46x speedup over gcc -O3. Directly relevant to YGN-SAGE's evolution engine for code optimization tasks. The RL fine-tuning approach for correctness could enhance the evolution pipeline's fitness evaluation. Shows frontier LLMs can meaningfully optimize already-optimized code.

title: Automating the Search for Artificial Life with Foundation Models
abstract: With the recent Nobel Prize awarded for radical advances in protein discovery, foundation models for exploring large combinatorial spaces promise to revolutionize many scientific fields. ASAL (Automated Search for Artificial Life) integrates vision-language foundation models into ALife research, discovering simulations that produce target phenomena, generate temporally open-ended novelty, and illuminate diverse simulation spaces across Boids, Particle Life, Game of Life, Lenia, and Neural Cellular Automata.
relevance: ASAL uses foundation models to drive open-ended search through ALife simulations. Relevant to YGN-SAGE's open-ended evolution aspirations -- the illumination of diverse simulation spaces parallels MAP-Elites topology search. The use of VLMs as fitness evaluators is an innovative pattern that could extend YGN-SAGE's evaluator capabilities beyond code metrics.

title: Open-Endedness is Essential for Artificial Superhuman Intelligence
abstract: In recent years there has been a tremendous surge in the general capabilities of AI systems, mainly fuelled by training foundation models on internet-scale data. Nevertheless, the creation of open-ended, ever self-improving AI remains elusive. In this position paper, we argue that the ingredients are now in place to achieve open-endedness in AI systems with respect to a human observer. Furthermore, we claim that such open-endedness is an essential property of any artificial superhuman intelligence (ASI). We provide a formal definition of open-endedness through novelty and learnability.
relevance: Position paper arguing open-endedness is essential for ASI. Provides formal definitions of open-endedness (novelty + learnability) that could formalize YGN-SAGE's evolution objectives. The vision of foundation-model-based open-ended systems aligns with YGN-SAGE's self-evolving architecture. From DeepMind researchers.

title: Self-Organized Agents: A LLM Multi-Agent Framework toward Ultra Large-Scale Code Generation and Optimization
abstract: Recent advancements in automatic code generation using large language model (LLM) agent have brought us closer to the future of automated software development. However, existing single-agent approaches face limitations in generating and improving large-scale, complex codebases due to constraints in context length. We propose Self-Organized multi-Agent framework (SoA), enabling scalable and efficient generation and optimization of large-scale code through independent agent operation and collaboration.
relevance: SoA's self-organizing multi-agent approach to large-scale code generation addresses scalability challenges relevant to YGN-SAGE. The automatic agent multiplication based on problem complexity parallels YGN-SAGE's dynamic sub-agent pool (agent_pool.py). Architecture for handling context-length limitations is practically useful.

title: Evolutionary Computation in the Era of Large Language Model: Survey and Roadmap
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have not only revolutionized natural language processing but also extended their prowess to various domains, marking a significant stride towards artificial general intelligence. The interplay between LLMs and evolutionary algorithms (EAs), despite differing in objectives and methodologies, share a common pursuit of applicability in complex problems. This comprehensive survey presents a forward-looking roadmap categorizing mutual inspiration into LLM-enhanced evolutionary optimization and EA-enhanced LLM.
relevance: Comprehensive survey mapping the LLM-EA intersection. Provides taxonomy of approaches relevant to all five YGN-SAGE pillars: LLM-enhanced optimization (evolution pillar), neural architecture search (topology), prompt engineering via EA (strategy), and code generation (tools). Essential reference for positioning YGN-SAGE in the field.

title: A Systematic Survey on Large Language Models for Algorithm Design
abstract: Algorithm design is crucial for effective problem-solving across various domains. The advent of Large Language Models (LLMs) has notably enhanced automation and innovation within this field. In just a few years, this integration has yielded remarkable progress in areas ranging from combinatorial optimization to scientific discovery. This systematic survey collected around 3,000 papers as of October 2025, providing a holistic understanding of LLMs for algorithm design.
relevance: Most comprehensive survey on LLMs for algorithm design (3000+ papers). Covers two paradigms directly relevant to YGN-SAGE: meta-algorithm generation (complete algorithm synthesis, like evolution engine) and component-level semantic reconstruction (like DGM mutations). Essential for understanding the competitive landscape.

title: A Survey of Self-Evolving Agents: What, When, How, and Where to Evolve on the Path to Artificial Super Intelligence
abstract: Large Language Models demonstrate capabilities across diverse tasks but remain fundamentally static, unable to adapt parameters to novel tasks or evolving contexts. This survey provides the first systematic review of self-evolving agents, organizing the field around three dimensions: what to evolve (agent components), when to evolve (adaptation stages), and how to evolve (algorithmic designs). It examines applications in coding, education, and healthcare.
relevance: First systematic survey of self-evolving agents -- the exact paradigm YGN-SAGE implements. The what/when/how framework directly maps to YGN-SAGE's five pillars. Provides taxonomy of evolutionary mechanisms, adaptation triggers, and algorithmic designs that can benchmark and improve YGN-SAGE's evolution engine.

title: A Comprehensive Survey of Self-Evolving AI Agents: A New Paradigm Bridging Foundation Models and Lifelong Agentic Systems
abstract: Recent advances in large language models have sparked growing interest in AI agents capable of solving complex, real-world tasks. However, most existing agent systems rely on manually crafted configurations that remain static after deployment, limiting their ability to adapt to dynamic and evolving environments. This survey explores agent evolution techniques that automatically enhance agent systems based on interaction data and environmental feedback, laying the foundation for self-evolving AI agents.
relevance: Complementary survey to 2507.21046, focusing on bridging foundation models with lifelong agentic systems. The evolution-from-interaction-data paradigm aligns with YGN-SAGE's memory-driven evolution. Covers agent evolution techniques that could be integrated into YGN-SAGE's evolution engine.

title: Lifelong Learning of Large Language Model based Agents: A Roadmap
abstract: Lifelong learning, also known as continual or incremental learning, is a crucial component for advancing Artificial General Intelligence (AGI) by enabling systems to continuously adapt in dynamic environments. While large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated impressive capabilities in natural language processing, existing LLM agents are typically designed for static systems. This paper systematically summarizes techniques for incorporating lifelong learning into LLM-based agents across perception, memory, and action modules.
relevance: Directly relevant to YGN-SAGE's memory pillar and lifelong learning capabilities. The three-module decomposition (perception, memory, action) maps to YGN-SAGE's architecture. Provides techniques for evolving knowledge stores, which could enhance ExoCortex and episodic memory components.

title: Evolutionary Generation of Multi-Agent Systems
abstract: Large language model (LLM)-based multi-agent systems (MAS) show strong promise for complex reasoning, planning, and tool-augmented tasks, but designing effective MAS architectures remains labor-intensive, brittle, and hard to generalize. Existing automatic MAS generation methods either rely on code generation, which often leads to executability and robustness failures, or impose rigid architectural constraints.
relevance: Most recent paper (Feb 2026) on evolutionary generation of multi-agent systems. Addresses the exact challenges YGN-SAGE faces: automated MAS architecture design, executability guarantees, and robustness. The critique of code-generation-based approaches is relevant to improving YGN-SAGE's topology evolution reliability.

title: AgentCoder: Multi-Agent-based Code Generation with Iterative Testing and Optimisation
abstract: The rise of large language models (LLMs) has sped up code generation, but challenges in producing accurate code remain. AgentCoder introduces a novel multi-agent framework with three specialized agents: programmer, test designer, and test executor. The test designer generates comprehensive test suites (basic, edge, large-scale), the programmer generates and refines code based on executor feedback. Achieves 91.5% pass@1 with GPT-4, significantly outperforming single-agent approaches.
relevance: The three-agent architecture (programmer/tester/executor) maps directly to YGN-SAGE's S2 AVR loop (Act-Verify-Refine). The iterative test-driven refinement pattern is a proven approach for improving code quality in evolutionary pipelines. Could enhance YGN-SAGE's evolution engine with structured test generation.

title: Mathematical discoveries from program search with large language models (FunSearch)
abstract: FunSearch (searching in the function space) is an evolutionary procedure that pairs a pretrained LLM with a systematic evaluator. It searches for programs that describe how to solve a problem rather than what the solution is. Applied to the cap set problem in extremal combinatorics, FunSearch discovers constructions that go beyond existing ones, achieving the largest improvement in 20 years to the asymptotic lower bound. Also finds new bin packing heuristics outperforming traditional baselines.
relevance: Seminal Nature paper establishing LLM+evolution for mathematical discovery. FunSearch's program-search-over-function-space paradigm is foundational to YGN-SAGE's evolution engine. Key innovations: islands model for population diversity, best-shot sampling, automated evaluation. The programs database with islands directly inspired evolutionary population management.
